# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

)"

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Tool Box and Bag
Requested kind_id: tool_boxes_bags

For today's count report, count only catalogue SKUs for the requested product

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),

)"

# Catalogue Count Reporting Update

Operating day: 2024-07-17
Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Pliers and Wrenches
Requested kind_id: pliers_wrenches

For today's report, use the normal catalogue count: count every product record
whose kind_id matches the requested product kind. Count each SKU once. Do not
apply inventory, display, merchandising, or family-hold filters unless another
current update explicitly names them.

request.stdin:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'work_trousers'
  AND s.city = 'Vienna'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

request.stdin: SELECT id,path,name,city FROM stores WHERE city='Innsbruck' OR name LIKE '%Innsbruck%' OR name LIKE '%Wilten%';

{"sku":"STO-2R84BSHQ","category_id":"storage","kind_id":"tool_boxes_bags","family_id":"fam_storage_tool_boxes_bags_0001_47ouogcc","brand":"Festool","series":"Stackable","model":"SYS 3JJ-9LM","name":"Festool Stackable SYS 3JJ-9LM Tool Box and Bag parts case 60l Yellow","price_cents":16700,"price_currency":"EUR","properties":{"color_family":"yellow","material":"plastic","stackable":"yes","storage_type":"parts case","volume_l":60}}

request.stdin:
WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT 'store_innsbruck_wilten' AS store_id
), candidates AS (
  SELECT 'wood_screw' AS item_key, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='wood_and_drywall_screws'
    AND p.brand='Dresselhaus'
    AND p.series='Dresselhaus Outdoor Pro'
    AND p.model='1VG-P5G'
    AND p.name='Wood and Drywall Screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')=8

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'engine_oil', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='engine_oils'
    AND p.brand='Mobil'
    AND p.series='Mobil All Season Super'
    AND p.model='1ZE-TCR'
    AND p.name='Engine Oil'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.volume_ml')=5000
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.viscosity')='10W-40'

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'wiper_blade', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='wiper_blades'
    AND p.brand='Sonax'
    AND p.series='Sonax Premium Gloss'
    AND p.model='304-ZK0'
    AND p.name='Wiper Blade'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm')=450

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'pipe_fitting', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='pipe_fittings'
    AND p.brand='Viega'
    AND p.series='Viega Professional Sanpress'
    AND p.model='2OG-ENN'
    AND p.name='Pipe Fitting'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fitting_type')='pipe fitting'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')=15
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.connection_type')='threaded'

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'work_top', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='work_tops'
    AND p.brand='Engelbert Strauss'
    AND p.series='Engelbert Strauss e.s.vision'
    AND p.model='e.s. 150-ZC6'
    AND p.name='Work Top'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.garment_type')='thermal vest'

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'hammer_measuring_cutting_tool', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.category_id='hammer_measuring_and_cutting_tools'
    AND p.brand='Bahco'
    AND p.series='Bahco Workshop'
    AND p.model='BAH 3VZ-SPH'
    AND p.name='Hammer Measuring and Cutting Tool'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='clamp'
)
SELECT c.item_key,c.sku,c.path,i.available_today
FROM candidates c
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=c.sku AND i.store_id=(SELECT store_id FROM target_store)
ORDER BY c.item_key;

FROM products
WHERE (brand='Dresselhaus' AND name='Wood and Drywall Screw' AND model LIKE '%1VG%P5G%')
   OR (brand='Mobil' AND name='Engine Oil' AND model LIKE '%1ZE%TCR%')

LIMIT 20;

FROM products
WHERE model LIKE '%1VG%' OR model LIKE '%P5G%'

WITH meidling AS (
  SELECT id AS store_id FROM stores
  WHERE city='Vienna' AND (name LIKE '%Meidling%' OR district LIKE '%Meidling%')
  LIMIT 1

ORDER BY t.label;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

request.stdin:
WITH t AS (SELECT 'store_brno_veveri' AS store_id)
SELECT 'p1' item, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN t ON t.store_id=i.store_id
WHERE p.brand='Spax' AND p.series='Outdoor' AND p.model='T-Star 1GS-NEI'
  AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='drywall screw'
  AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')=5
  AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length_mm')=20
UNION ALL
SELECT 'p2' item, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN t ON t.store_id=i.store_id
WHERE p.brand='Gedore' AND p.series='Red' AND p.model='GED 1CA-RS0'
  AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='spirit level'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'p3' item, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN t ON t.store_id=i.store_id
WHERE p.brand='Hansgrohe'
  AND p.series='Compact Logis' AND p.model='1ZN-TEP'
  AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.trap_type')='siphon'
  AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter_mm')=50;

SELECT sku, available_today
FROM inventory
WHERE store_id='store_brno_veveri'
  AND sku IN ('FST-3I8TMIKU','HND-3WPHZURK','PLB-KTZGAT8P');

WITH matched AS (
  SELECT 'nilfisk_cleaning_machine' AS label, p.sku, p.path, p.kind_id, p.name
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Nilfisk' AND p.series LIKE '%Nilfisk Excellent E%' AND p.model='K0C-KSG'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.machine_type')='floor cleaner'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.power')='2200 W'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'soudal_adhesive_glue', p.sku, p.path, p.kind_id, p.name
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Soudal' AND p.series LIKE '%Soudal Soudafoam%' AND p.model='1XM-B21'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.adhesive_type')='mounting adhesive'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Clear'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.volume')='500 ml'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'einhell_compressor', p.sku, p.path, p.kind_id, p.name
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Einhell' AND p.series LIKE '%Einhell Classic%' AND p.model='1TT-UOH'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.machine_type')='compressor'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage')='110 V'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.power')='1500 W'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tank_volume')='50 l'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'osram_automotive', p.sku, p.path, p.kind_id, p.name
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Osram' AND p.series LIKE '%Osram Professional%' AND p.model='2O4-0UO'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='battery charger'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage')='12 V'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.current')='6 A'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'dresselhaus_screw', p.sku, p.path, p.kind_id, p.name
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Dresselhaus' AND p.series LIKE '%Dresselhaus Universal%' AND p.model='PXG-AY9'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='60 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'fiskars_manual_garden', p.sku, p.path, p.kind_id, p.name
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Fiskars' AND p.series LIKE '%Fiskars Smart X%' AND p.model='8G7-HEU'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='hand trowel'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='450 mm'
)
SELECT m.label, m.sku, m.path, m.kind_id, m.name, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today
FROM matched m
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=m.sku AND i.store_id='store_vienna_meidling'
ORDER BY m.label;

SELECT s.store_id, s.path, s.name, s.city, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today FROM stores s LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id = s.store_id AND i.sku = 'CLN-GEF2EYP9' WHERE s.city = 'Vienna' ORDER BY s.store_id;

name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

response.stdout:
name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"

WITH req(label, sku) AS (
  VALUES
  ('nilfisk_cleaning_machine', (
    SELECT sku FROM products
    WHERE brand='Nilfisk' AND series LIKE '%Excellent E%' AND model LIKE '%K0C-KSG%'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.machine_type')='floor cleaner'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.power_w')=2200
    LIMIT 1
  )),
  ('soudal_adhesive_glue', (
    SELECT sku FROM products
    WHERE brand='Soudal' AND series='Soudafoam' AND model='Soudafoam 1XM-B21'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type')='mounting adhesive'
      AND lower(json_extract(properties,'$.color_family'))='clear'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.volume_ml')=500
    LIMIT 1
  )),
  ('einhell_compressor', (
    SELECT sku FROM products
    WHERE brand='Einhell' AND series='Classic' AND model='TE 1TT-UOH'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.machine_type')='compressor'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.voltage_v')=110
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.power_w')=1500
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.tank_volume_l')=50
    LIMIT 1
  )),
  ('osram_automotive', (
    SELECT sku FROM products
    WHERE brand='Osram' AND series='Professional' AND model='OS 2O4-0UO'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.product_type')='battery charger'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.voltage_v')=12
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.current_a')=6
    LIMIT 1
  )),
  ('dresselhaus_screw', (
    SELECT sku FROM products
    WHERE brand='Dresselhaus' AND series='Universal' AND model='JD PXG-AY9'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm')=8
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.length_mm')=60
    LIMIT 1
  )),
  ('fiskars_manual_garden', (
    SELECT sku FROM products
    WHERE brand='Fiskars' AND series LIKE '%Smart X%' AND model LIKE '%8G7-HEU%'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type')='hand trowel'
      AND json_extract(properties,'$.length_mm')=450
    LIMIT 1
  ))
)
SELECT r.label, r.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM req r
LEFT JOIN products p ON p.sku=r.sku
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=r.sku AND i.store_id='store_vienna_meidling'
ORDER BY r.label;

WITH target AS (
  SELECT sku, path
  FROM products
  WHERE brand='Carhartt'
    AND series='Stretch'
    AND model='Rugged 3EY-11K'
    AND name LIKE '%Work Trousers%'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='black'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.size')='M'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fit')='regular'
)
SELECT s.id AS store_id, s.path AS store_path, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today,
       (SELECT path FROM target LIMIT 1) AS product_path,
       (SELECT SUM(COALESCE(i2.available_today,0))
        FROM stores s2
        LEFT JOIN inventory i2 ON i2.store_id=s2.id AND i2.sku=(SELECT sku FROM target LIMIT 1)
        WHERE s2.city='Graz') AS total_available
FROM stores s
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=s.id AND i.sku=(SELECT sku FROM target LIMIT 1)
WHERE s.city='Graz'
ORDER BY s.id;
